candidateFor: International patients with atrophic, boxcar, or rolling acne scars seeking visible improvement without surgery
Patients with skin types III–V who need scar protocols designed to minimize post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation
Those committed to 3–6 monthly sessions and able to schedule 1–2 treatments during their Korea visit
Patients whose scarring has plateaued and topical treatments no longer improve appearance
Individuals seeking combination-protocol treatments (laser, microneedling, subcision) unavailable or prohibitively expensive in their home countries

whatToExpect.detail: Dermatologist examines scar depth, type (atrophic, boxcar, rolling, icepick), and skin tone. Customizes treatment plan combining fractional laser, microneedling RF, subcision, or chemical peels. Discusses realistic timelines: visible softening after 2–3 sessions; full results over 3–6 months.

Topical numbing cream applied; sometimes local anesthetic injected for subcision. Fractional laser or RF microneedling targets scar tissue; subcision mechanically releases fibrous tethering. Minimal discomfort with anesthesia. Single session initiates remodeling; cumulative effect over subsequent treatments.

Plan 3–7 days in Korea post-treatment for initial swelling to subside before travel. Sun protection mandatory (SPF 50+). Monthly maintenance sessions recommended; schedule second treatment 1 month after first. Most patients tolerate downtime well; resume normal activity after 1 week. Results progressively improve over 3–6 months.

whyKorea: Korean dermatologists specialize in scar protocols designed for Asian skin phototypes, using MFDS-approved devices and techniques that significantly reduce post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation risk compared to aggressive Western approaches

Combination protocols—fractional laser, RF microneedling, subcision, and chemical peels performed by single provider—integrate multiple modalities in single visit, accelerating results unavailable as separate procedures in most countries
Treatment costs USD 150–600 per session, approximately 40–70% below comparable Western private practice pricing, making multi-session protocols financially feasible for international patients
Seoul clinics equipped with latest fractional laser (Fraxel, Picosure) and RF microneedling systems; advanced technology combined with high-volume scar patient experience ensures optimal outcomes

faqs.question: How many sessions will I need to see real results?
faqs.answer: Most protocols require 3–6 monthly sessions for visible cumulative improvement. A single Korea visit can accommodate 1–2 initial sessions; return visits or home-country maintenance complete the series. Noticeable softening typically appears after 2–3 treatments over 2–3 months.

faqs.question: Can I fly home immediately after treatment?
faqs.answer: Plan 3–7 days in Korea post-treatment for swelling to subside. Flight cabin pressure and air travel do not delay healing, but visible redness may persist during travel. Sun protection during flight is essential (window shade, SPF 50+ lip balm, hat).

faqs.question: Will the procedure work on all scar types?
faqs.answer: Fractional laser, RF microneedling, and subcision each address different scar architectures. Boxcar and rolling scars respond well to combination protocols. Icepick scars may require additional subcision or TCA cross. Dermatologist customizes approach per individual scar pattern during consultation.

faqs.question: What if I have darker skin? Will treatment cause hyperpigmentation?
faqs.answer: Korean dermatologists routinely treat patients with skin types III–V using lower laser fluences, longer healing intervals, and RF microneedling (lower hyperpigmentation risk than ablative lasers). Strict sun avoidance post-treatment prevents post-inflammatory pigmentation changes. Discuss your skin history during consultation.

faqs.question: How long until results last?
faqs.answer: Acne scar remodeling is permanent—collagen restructuring does not reverse. Results continue improving 6–12 months post-treatment. Maintenance sessions every 12–24 months address ongoing skin aging and prevent new scarring, but initial improvements are durable.
